title: Xenial Vietnamese Nectarines Bun Thit Nuong
description: A romantic, sour and fluffy Vietnamese dish that pairs sweet nectarines with grilled kosher chicken in a fondue pot.
instructions: In a bowl whisk together flour, water, sugar, fish sauce, soy sauce, garlic powder, salt and lime juice until smooth batter forms.
Heat a fondue pot over medium heat and add vegetable oil.
Dip each chicken strip into the batter, letting excess drip off, then place in the hot oil.
Fry the coated chicken until golden brown and cooked through, about 5 minutes, flipping halfway.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside on a paper towel to drain.
In the same fondue pot add the diced nectarines and a splash of water, sautéing until they soften slightly, about 3 minutes.
Sprinkle the cooked chicken back over the nectarines and stir gently to combine.
Drizzle a little lime juice over the mixture and season with black pepper if desired.
Serve the nectarines and chicken hot, garnished with fresh herbs like mint or cilantro if available.
Enjoy this romantic, sour and fluffy Vietnamese dish with a side of rice or a fresh salad.
